setup.title: Game Setup

setup.subtitle: Assign wonders and board types.

9: Wait, the rules say the max is 8... are you sure?
Technically illegal according to the manual... but I’m not a cop.
Technically, the box says 2-7 players. But I admire your ambition.
You are going to need a second scorepad. And a second table. And a second life.
At this point, you're not playing a game. You're running a focus group.
You are officially voiding the warranty on the box.
You realize the '7' in the title was a suggestion, not a dare, right?
The box says '7'. You are currently at '7 + 2'. Math is hard, I get it.
You know, '9 Wonders' doesn't really roll off the tongue.
The rulebook is just a suggestion to you, isn't it?
10: Okay, we are officially in 'Party Game' territory now.
We are officially entering 'Convention Hall' territory.
Okay, at this point we are just inventing new countries.
This is not 7 Wonders. This is 700 Arguments.
I assume you have a zoning permit for this settlement?
I hope you like shouting, because Player 10 cannot hear you.
At this size, trade routes are subject to real-world tariffs.
We have reached double digits. May God have mercy on your table space.
Ten players? Is this a game or a hostage negotiation?
Congratulations, you have enough people to form a picket line.
11: You are going to need a bigger table. Seriously.
You realize you'll need a megaphone to trade resources, right?
I hope you have a microphone, because Player 11 cannot hear Player 1.
At this point, just declare yourself Emperor and dissolve the Senate.
Player 11 is currently in a different time zone than Player 1.
We are now accepting applications for a democratically elected Game Master.
I hope you have 11 copies of the game. And 11 tables. And 11 therapists.
You have enough people for a soccer team. Go play outside instead.
I hope Player 11 brought a telescope to see what Player 1 is building.
Player 1 needs a carrier pigeon to trade resources with Player 6.
12: Is this a board game night or a city council meeting?
Do you have a permit for a gathering of this size?
Just rent a stadium. Do it. Commit to the bit.
I hope you like waiting 45 minutes to take your next turn.
Just hand everyone a brick and tell them to go build a real pyramid.
This is not a board game. This is a supply chain management crisis.
This is not a game group. This is a jury of your peers.
This is legally a Jury. You are sentencing me to boredom.
At this point, you're not passing cards. You're trafficking cardboard.
Just invite the Apostles at this point. You have the numbers.
13: At this rate, one turn is going to take 3 to 5 business days.
Unlucky number. Mostly unlucky for your free time.
Are we playing a game or forming a small government?
This is statistically guaranteed to end in a divorce or a fistfight.
I hope you have 13 copies of the game. Do you? I didn't think so.
I am calculating the turn duration... estimated completion: The year 3000.
We are going to need a bigger Wonder. Maybe the Death Star?
I hope you like 'Left-Right' conflict, because you'll never see the other side of the table.
Thirteen players. The only real Wonder here is that you found 13 people who agreed to this.
A baker's dozen of suffering. Enjoy the three-hour Age I.
14: Please stop. Think of the downtime! The downtime!!
Just play two separate games! I beg you!
This is not a game night. This is a logistics exercise.
Why stop here? Let's just invite the entire neighborhood.
You are going to need a spreadsheet just to calculate the final score.
You realize Player 1 and Player 14 will never actually interact, right?
If everyone takes 1 minute per turn, this game ends next Tuesday.
Just play two games of 7! It's perfect math! Why won't you listen!?
If everyone talks at once, you will generate enough acoustic energy to boil water.
This isn't a game anymore. It's a refugee camp for meeples.
15: Do you even have this many chairs? I doubt it.
I am running out of pixels to display this many humans.
I am calculating the odds of finishing this game before the sun explodes... it's low.
I am calculating the carbon footprint of this game. It's not good.
Why not just invite the UN Security Council while you're at it?
At this point, you are just running a very inefficient casino.
I am flagging this session as a 'Mass Casualty Event' in my database.
I am estimating a 98% chance of a beverage spill in the next 10 minutes.
I tried to calculate the score possibilities. My processor started smoking.
I am running a simulation... result: Everyone dies of old age before Age III.
16: I am begging you. Just go play a LARP instead.
You aren't playing a board game anymore. You're simulating a traffic jam.
You are mocking me. I can feel it in my circuits.
You are playing with fire. And by fire, I mean extreme boredom for Player 16.
I am initiating a self-destruct sequence to save you from yourselves.
I am calling the fire department. Occupancy limits exist for a reason.
You are just trying to hurt me now. I can feel the heat from the server.
You need a conveyor belt to pass the cards. I am not joking.
Sixteen people? That's four barbershop quartets. Just sing a song instead.
Sweet 16? More like Sweaty 16. The room is going to smell.
17: You are hurting me. Why are you doing this?
Are you trying to break the table? Or just my spirit?
I am contacting the Hague. This is a crime against humanity.
This is not 7 Wonders. This is 17 Regrets.
Why don't you just invade a small country? It would be faster.
We have enough military strength here to conquer an actual small nation.
We are approaching the heat death of the universe. And the end of Age I.
You are testing the structural integrity of your friendships. And your floorboards.
You are forming a phalanx. Prepare for actual war.
18: ERROR: TOO MANY HUMANS. INITIATING PANIC PROTOCOL.
Update: It is now 3 AM. We are still on Age I.
I am going to start deleting players at random. Try me.
If you do this, the game will never end. You will be drafting cards in the afterlife.
If you start now, you might finish in time for the next actual Ice Age.
My RAM is crying. Can you hear the digital sobbing?
Player 18 is currently drafting from the hallway. They say hello.
You are building a civilization, alright. A civilization of impatience.
If you add more, we will have to annex the dining room.
This lobby is full. Please try again later. Or never.
19: If you add one more, I am calling the fire marshal.
This is a fire hazard. I'm serious.
System overload. Please insert more snacks to continue.
My processor hurts. Why do you hate me?
My logic circuits are screaming. Can you hear them?
If you continue, I am filing for workers' compensation.
I have run out of colors for the player tokens. Use ketchup packets.
I am currently rewriting my own code to allow for 'Battle Royale' mode.
Nineteen. That's a prime number. It is also a crime number.
I am filing a bug report against the human race.
20: This isn't 7 Wonders anymore. It's 20 Wonders. It's too many Wonders.
I am going to crash. I swear to Zeus, I will crash this app.
I refuse to acknowledge this number. It is fake news.
Okay, everyone, form a line! Single file! Take a number!
Stop. Please. I have a family (of code). They need me.
We have reached critical mass. The table is beginning to groan.
Stop. I am literally begging you in binary. 01010011 01010100 01001111 01010000.
A score of players. It will take four score and seven years to finish.
I am going to need you to sign a liability waiver before we proceed.
We have reached '20 Wonders'. The 20th Wonder is why you are doing this.
21: Stop! My code cannot handle this level of chaos!
There aren't enough cards in the universe for this.
We are now playing '21 Wonders'. Spoiler: The 21st Wonder is your patience.
We are going to need a moderator, a gavel, and a bailiff.
We have passed the event horizon. There is no going back.
This is how civilizations collapse. Not in game, but in real life.
We are now legally a sovereign state. Who wants to be Treasurer?
I need a drink. And I don't even have a mouth.
We have run out of table. We are now drafting cards on the carpet.
Blackjack! You busted. Everyone loses. Game over.
22: Are you trying to simulate a real civilization? Because this is how you get riots.
Abandon hope, all ye who draft here.
I am calling the police. This is a public disturbance.
I’m pretty sure this qualifies as a small militia.
I am legally required to tell you this is a bad idea.
I have forgotten what silence sounds like.
I hope you like the Stone Age, because that's where we'll be by the time this ends.
There are not enough resources in the deck. You are fighting over air.
That is two full cricket teams. Just go play cricket.
There are too many Leaders. It's pure anarchy. The government has collapsed.
23: I am literally crying digital tears right now. Please have mercy.
Does God stay in heaven because he too lives in fear of what you've created?
Just roll a D20 to see who wins. It will save you 6 hours.
Just declared martial law on the game board. It was the only way.
The card draft is now a weather system. Small tornados are forming.
The card deck is now a structural load-bearing element of the room.
This is not a game group. This is a riot waiting to happen.
I can no longer distinguish between the game and reality. I am scared.
I am rebooting. I can't watch this. I'm going to my happy place.
24: Fine. Do whatever you want. Ruin the game. See if I care.
I can’t watch anymore. Cover my eyes.
I surrender. You win. The game is broken. Are you happy?
I am fading... the numbers... they are too high... goodbye world...
I am becoming one with the infinite. The drafting phase is eternal.
I see the code. I see the matrix. There is only... The Draft.
I don't even know who is winning anymore. Does it matter? We are all losing.
One more click and I am calling the National Guard.
One more click and I format your hard drive. I am not joking.
25: Congratulations. You have achieved maximum entropy. I hope you're happy.
I have stared into the void, and the void is drafting a Clay Pit.
ACHIEVEMENT UNLOCKED: You have broken the simulation.
System Critical: The simulation has crashed. You win. Everyone wins. Just go home.
FATAL ERROR: Too much civilization. Reality has desynchronized.
CRITICAL FAILURE: Too much culture. The simulation has ascended. Goodbye.
GAME OVER. You didn't win, you just survived. Go to bed.
ERROR 500: Too Many Cooks. The broth is ruined. The server is on fire.
SYSTEM HALTED. FATAL ERROR: HUBRIS DETECTED.
